seed: control vs surrender; overflow_vs_containment; overfilled_center; stutter_step; hinge against negative_cross; thick_outline; pressure_vs_release; compass; lonely_signal | abstract variation request 75; target batch count 500
title: Structured Fracture
reasoning: The emotional tone of this composition reflects the tension between control and surrender, embodying the struggle of structured order overshadowed by chaotic flow. The selected palette of black, electric blue, magenta, and lime creates a vivid contrast; black represents control and structure, while the brighter colors signify the freedom and unpredictability of surrender. The main collision occurs in the center, where intense colors disrupt a more subdued background, symbolizing the overwhelming pressure versus the quiet release of letting go. This setup encourages the viewer to consider the dance between rigidity and fluidity, resonating with the seed's themes. Broken pixels introduce an element of imperfection, underscoring the idea that control can often be fractured. Ultimately, this abstract piece instills a sense of balance disrupted, a visual dialogue of opposing forces in a confined space.

layout.name: mirror_broken
layout.description: Left and right sides almost mirror, but 2 to 5 pixels are wrong.

composition.name: memory_vs_motion
composition.idea: Faded repeated marks are interrupted by a cleaner advancing path.

referenceExample.title: Resilient Scar
referenceExample.pattern: thin broken structure crossed by a repaired vertical path

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
